questions.question: What is a full-bleed layout?
questions.explanation: Full-bleed means content extends to the edges of the viewport without container padding.

questions.question: How do you create an overlapping grid layout?
questions.explanation: Grid spanning combined with margins/transform creates overlapping layout effects.

questions.question: What effect do full-screen hero backgrounds use?
questions.explanation: object-fit: cover ensures the background image fills the hero while maintaining aspect ratio.

questions.question: How do agency sites make typography bold?
questions.explanation: Agency sites use heavy font weights (900) and large clamp-generated font sizes for impact.

questions.question: What is the 'hero scroll' indicator?
questions.explanation: A subtle animated text or arrow at the bottom of the hero encourages the user to scroll.

questions.question: How do you create the hover card reveal effect?
questions.explanation: A pseudo-element with transform: translateX that slides on hover creates the reveal effect.

questions.question: What design aesthetic suits creative agencies?
questions.explanation: Agency sites often use minimal designs with a bold accent color for contrast and focus.

questions.question: How do you make nav links underline on hover?
questions.explanation: A pseudo-element with width: 0 that transitions to 100% on hover creates the underline effect.

questions.question: What ARIA role do decorative background images need?
questions.explanation: Decorative images should be hidden from screen readers with aria-hidden='true'.

questions.question: How do portfolio overlays typically appear?
questions.explanation: Overlays use opacity transitions for smooth hover reveals.

questions.question: How do you create a parallax scrolling effect on hero sections?
options: background-attachment: fixed on the hero background
questions.explanation: background-attachment: fixed creates a simple parallax effect where the background stays fixed during scroll.

questions.question: What CSS creates a marquee text effect for agency sites?
questions.explanation: A CSS keyframe animation that translates text from 100% to -100% creates the infinite marquee scroll.

questions.question: How do you create sticky section headers that persist during scroll?
options: position: sticky with top offset on section headings
questions.explanation: position: sticky on section title elements keeps them visible while their section is in view.

questions.question: What layout creates a contact form for agency websites?
options: CSS Grid with label/input pairs in a responsive layout
questions.explanation: A CSS Grid or flexbox layout with label-input pairs, styled inputs, and a submit button creates clean forms.

questions.question: How do you create a project filter system for portfolio items?
options: Filter buttons with data attributes and JS class toggling
questions.explanation: Filter buttons with data attributes and JavaScript toggling visibility classes is the standard portfolio filter pattern.

questions.question: What CSS creates the section dividers between agency sections?
options: Padding blocks with decorative pseudo-elements or angled clip-path
questions.explanation: Section dividers created with padding, pseudo-elements, or clip-path add visual separation between sections.

questions.question: How do you create numbered sections for 'About' pages?
options: Large pseudo-element numbers with relative positioning behind content
questions.explanation: Large pseudo-element numbers positioned behind or beside content create striking numbered section designs.

questions.question: What is the best approach for a video background on hero sections?
options: Position: absolute video element with object-fit: cover
questions.explanation: An absolute-positioned <video> element with object-fit: cover is the standard video background approach.

questions.question: How do you create a cursor follower effect for agency sites?
options: JavaScript tracking with a fixed-position div and transform updates
questions.explanation: A fixed-position div that follows the mouse via JavaScript mousemove events creates the cursor follower effect.

questions.question: What CSS creates the loading animation for portfolio images?
options: Keyframe animation with background gradient shimmer (skeleton screen)
questions.explanation: A CSS keyframe animation with a moving gradient shimmer creates the skeleton loading placeholder effect.
